 {"id":"wa-xfgv","title":"FTUI-09.3.a Compile-time guardrails against ratatui reintroduction","description":"## Background
Migration completion is fragile without guardrails that prevent ratatui/crossterm regressions from re-entering changed paths.

## Deliverables
- compile-time/static checks that fail when forbidden imports appear in migration-complete modules
- allowlist/exception mechanism with explicit expiry rationale
- developer guidance for resolving violations and selecting ftui-native alternatives

## Acceptance Criteria
- guardrails run in CI and locally with deterministic pass/fail output
- violation logs clearly identify file/module and remediation path
- policy prevents silent backsliding while allowing narrowly scoped, documented exceptions.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:14:44.745714205Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.216674-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-09T05:34:40.9487521Z"}
 {"id":"wa-xkcj","title":"Pane bookmarks + aliases","description":"## What
Allow users to bookmark panes with friendly names and optional tags.

## Why
Operators need fast access to critical panes without guessing IDs.

## How
- Storage for bookmarks (pane_id, alias, tags)
- CLI: `wa panes bookmark add/list/remove`
- Filters by alias/tag in status/search
- Add storage migrations via wa-y6g if new tables are required

## Success Criteria
- Bookmarks persist across restarts
- Alias collisions are handled clearly","notes":"2026-02-08: Added missing bookmark alias/tag filtering to wa status and wa search in crates/wa/src/main.rs, including multi-pane tag search merge behavior and CLI parser tests (cli_search_parses_bookmark_filters, cli_status_parses_bookmark_filters). Validation: cargo fmt --check passed; cargo check -p wa --all-targets passed; cargo clippy -p wa --all-targets -- -D warnings passed; cargo test -p wa bookmark_filters passed. Workspace-wide all-target checks currently fail due pre-existing wa-core test-target issues in crates/wa-core/src/config.rs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:14:45.866802819Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T01:34:52.132778-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-11T01:34:52.132795-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"wa-xkcj","depends_on_id":"wa-y6g","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T00:22:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"wa-xohn","title":"FTUI-08.1 Capture pre/post migration performance baselines","description":"## Background\nWe need objective evidence that migration does not degrade operator responsiveness.\n\n## Deliverables\n- baseline metrics from ratatui path\n- post-migration metrics from ftui path\n- comparison report with interpretation and hotspots\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- metrics are reproducible and versioned\n- performance deltas are clearly explained.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:09:04.093410353Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-11T00:47:48.32649-05:00","closed_at":"2026-02-09T10:06:34.596860088Z"}

 {"id":"wa-y1ps","title":"E2E script: pane exclude filter prevents capture (ignored pane absent from search, shown in status)","description":"# Task: E2E script — pane exclude filter

## Goal
Validate that pane selection filters protect privacy and behave predictably:
- excluded panes are visible (as ignored) but not ingested
- excluded panes do not appear in search results

## Scenario
- Spawn two dummy panes:
  1) "observed" pane prints OBSERVED_TOKEN
  2) "ignored" pane prints SECRET_TOKEN and has a title/cwd that matches an exclude rule

- Configure wa with an exclude rule that matches the ignored pane.

Steps
1) Start wa watch with the configured workspace.
2) Wait until the observed token is persisted (via wa robot search).
3) Assert that searching for SECRET_TOKEN returns no results.
4) Assert wa status / wa robot state shows the ignored pane as ignored with a reason.

## Assertions
- Observed pane is captured and searchable.
- Ignored pane is not captured and not searchable.
- Status output makes the ignore decision obvious.

## Artifacts
- Effective config used (including pane rules).
- wa watch logs with decision messages (no secrets).
- wa robot state JSON.
- wa robot search JSON for both tokens.

## Registry
- Register this case in the E2E case registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`), including prerequisites and default inclusion status.
- Ensure `./scripts/e2e_test.sh --all` includes this case (or mark non-default with justification).

## Logging
- Follow the standard E2E harness logging contract (`wa-4vx.10.6`) and runner (`wa-4vx.10.11`).
- Emit per-step start/end markers with timestamps + durations.
- Include case name and pane ids in every log line.
- Redact secrets (device codes, tokens) in logs and artifacts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test is deterministic.
- On failure, artifacts show whether the bug is:
  - filter matching
  - ingest mistakenly tailing ignored panes
  - search indexing
  - status/state reporting


## Testing
- Meta-validation:
  - Assert that SECRET_TOKEN never appears in artifacts/logs (privacy guarantee), not just “not searchable”.
  - Add a control assertion that OBSERVED_TOKEN does appear in search to avoid false negatives.

- Artifact validation:
  - Assert state/status artifacts include the ignore reason so the user can understand why capture was skipped.

 {"id":"wa-y2e","title":"Robot mode dry-run: wa robot … --dry-run previews (send/workflow/events)","description":"# Robot mode dry-run previews (`wa robot … --dry-run`)

## Goal
Extend dry-run preview semantics to robot-mode commands so an AI agent can plan safely before taking actions.

This task is specifically about **robot mode** (machine output), and complements the **human** dry-run work in `wa-1pe.*`.

## Scope
Add `--dry-run` to robot commands that would otherwise mutate state:
- `wa robot send --dry-run …`
- `wa robot workflow … --dry-run`

Also add “planning previews” for read-only commands that *cause the agent to act*:
- `wa robot events --would-handle --dry-run` (preview which workflows would run, without running them)

Non-goals (for v0.1):
- actually granting approvals from robot mode (approval is intentionally a human/interactive surface)

## Output contract
- Uses the standard robot envelope (`wa-4vx.7.1`, schemas in `wa-4vx.7.10`).
- Must be deterministic and redacted.
- Must never:
  - send text
  - acquire locks/reservations
  - mutate DB state (other than unavoidable read-side effects like opening the DB)

## Examples

### `wa robot send --dry-run`
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "data": {
    "command": "send",
    "dry_run": true,
    "target": {
      "pane_id": 3,
      "title": "codex @ /project",
      "agent_type": "codex"
    },
    "input": {
      "text_preview": "continue",
      "text_len": 8
    },
    "policy_evaluation": {
      "decision": "allow",
      "capabilities": {
        "prompt_active": true,
        "command_running": false,
        "alt_screen": false,
        "recent_gap": false
      },
      "command_safety": {
        "is_command_candidate": false,
        "decision": "allow"
      }
    },
    "would_execute": true
  },
  "error": null
}
```

### `wa robot workflow run --dry-run`
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "data": {
    "command": "workflow_run",
    "dry_run": true,
    "workflow": "handle_compaction",
    "target": {"pane_id": 3, "title": "claude @ /project"},
    "steps_preview": [
      {"step": 1, "name": "verify_compaction_state", "kind": "check_pattern"},
      {"step": 2, "name": "inject_context", "kind": "send_text", "text_preview": "Please re-read …"},
      {"step": 3, "name": "wait_ack", "kind": "wait_for", "pattern": "I've reviewed"}
    ],
    "estimated_duration_ms": 30000
  },
  "error": null
}
```

### `wa robot events --would-handle --dry-run`
```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "data": {
    "command": "events",
    "dry_run": true,
    "events": [
      {
        "event_id": 123,
        "rule_id": "codex.usage_limit_reached",
        "pane_id": 9,
        "would_handle_with": {
          "workflow": "handle_usage_limits",
          "first_step": "exit_codex",
          "estimated_duration_ms": 60000
        }
      }
    ]
  },
  "error": null
}
```

## Design notes
- Robot dry-run should reuse the same planning logic as human dry-run where possible:
  - shared `DryRunReport` structs (different renderers)
  - same PolicyEngine evaluation paths
- If `--dry-run` is combined with `--wait-for` style arguments, the preview should include:
  - what matcher would be used
  - timeout/backoff parameters
  - but must not actually wait

## Testing
- Unit/contract tests:
  - `--dry-run` never calls SendText / workflow execution
  - outputs validate against JSON schemas (`wa-4vx.7.10`)
  - redaction invariants hold (no secrets in previews)

- E2E:
  - add an adapter case to the E2E registry (`wa-4vx.10.20`) that runs:
    - `wa robot send --dry-run …`
    - `wa robot workflow … --dry-run`
    - `wa robot events --would-handle --dry-run`
  - assert:
    - outputs are parseable JSON
    - `dry_run: true`
    - no audit “action executed” rows were created

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `wa robot send --dry-run` returns a deterministic, schema-valid preview including policy evaluation.
- [ ] `wa robot workflow … --dry-run` returns a deterministic, schema-valid step preview.
- [ ] `wa robot events --would-handle --dry-run` previews which workflows would run.
- [ ] Dry-run paths do not acquire locks or mutate panes/DB state.

 {"id":"wa-y3gp","title":"Design: storage/indexing perf targets, metrics, and budgets","description":"# Task: Design storage/indexing perf targets

## Goal
Define measurable targets and budgets for storage and indexing.

## Requirements
- Define scale targets:
  - panes: N (e.g., 50+)
  - transcript size: M (e.g., multi-GB)
  - ingest rate: bytes/sec
- Define latency budgets:
  - append segment p95
  - FTS query p95 for common queries
  - indexing lag ceiling
- Define health metrics:
  - writer queue depth
  - WAL size / checkpoint cadence
  - index progress per pane

## Acceptance Criteria
- Targets are explicit and can be enforced by benchmarks and/or E2E.
